stm32g474.note: Common STM32G474 pin functions; verify the exact package and alternate-function mapping before PCB layout.
power_ground: VDDA / VSSA: analog supply and analog ground
VREF+: ADC/DAC reference input or VREFBUF output

SPI: SPI1 PA5 SCK / PA6 MISO / PA7 MOSI
SPI2 PB13 SCK / PB14 MISO / PB15 MOSI
ADC: Many analog-capable pins: PA0-PA7, PB0-PB1, PC0-PC5

standout_pins: PB8-BOOT0: boot strap pin; also has I2C1_SCL and FDCAN1_RX alternate functions
PG10-NRST: reset pin on packages that expose it
PA13 SWDIO / PA14 SWCLK: debug pins after reset
PB4 / PB6: UCPD CC pins with startup dead-battery pull-down behavior

esp32c3.note: ESP32-C3 peripherals are mostly GPIO-matrix routable; defaults below are common fixed IO_MUX or dev-board choices.
power_ground: CHIP_EN / EN: chip enable and reset input

UART: UART0 GPIO21 TX / GPIO20 RX by IO_MUX
UART1 can be routed through the GPIO matrix
SPI: SPI2/FSPI IO_MUX: GPIO6 CLK, GPIO7 MOSI, GPIO2 MISO, GPIO10 CS
GPIO12-GPIO17 are usually used by SPI flash and are not recommended for general IO
I2C: I2C can be routed through the GPIO matrix
Common dev-board choice: GPIO8 SDA / GPIO9 SCL
CAN: TWAI controller TX/RX can be routed through the GPIO matrix

standout_pins: GPIO2, GPIO8, GPIO9: strapping pins; avoid hard pulls that fight boot mode
GPIO9: commonly connected to BOOT button on dev boards
GPIO18 / GPIO19: USB Serial/JTAG by default
